repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
I wanted to try implementing c11-std-atomics into cython since free-threading is going to become extremely common in the future and users likewise should have some way of easily access to atomic apis in C and not just in C++. 

My other suggestion or workaround would be to make a header file similar to pyatomics but for Cython that could have unified support for gcc, msvc intrinsics, and std.

> [!WARNING]
> I have no idea if fused templating is going to work or not but it was worth a shot so for now this pr is a draft.

comment_data.body: I don't think you really need the `"_Atomic(_Bool)"` cnames here. Since the definitions for `atomic_bool` are already in the stdatomic header
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,213 @@
+# https://en.cppreference.com/c/header/stdatomic
+
+from .stddef cimport wchar_t, ptrdiff_t
+from .stdint cimport uintptr_t, intptr_t, intmax_t, uintmax_t
+
+cdef extern from "<stdatomic.h>" nogil:
+    ctypedef short char16_t
+    ctypedef int char32_t
+
+    ctypedef enum memory_order:
+        memory_order_relaxed,
+        memory_order_consume,
+        memory_order_acquire,
+        memory_order_release,
+        memory_order_acq_rel,
+        memory_order_seq_cst
+
+    int ATOMIC_BOOL_LOCK_FREE
+    int ATOMIC_CHAR_LOCK_FREE
+    int ATOMIC_CHAR16_T_LOCK_FREE
+    int ATOMIC_CHAR32_T_LOCK_FREE
+    int ATOMIC_WCHAR_T_LOCK_FREE
+    int ATOMIC_SHORT_LOCK_FREE
+    int ATOMIC_INT_LOCK_FREE
+    int ATOMIC_LONG_LOCK_FREE
+    int ATOMIC_LLONG_LOCK_FREE
+    int ATOMIC_POINTER_LOCK_FREE
+
+    ctypedef bint atomic_bool "_Atomic(_Bool)"

commit.message: implement some form of stdatomics for C
